hawkish_phrases: Some participants saw the most likely appropriate path of monetary policy as involving no reductions in the target range for the federal funds rate this year
upside risks to inflation remained meaningful in light of factors such as elevated short-term inflation expectations
Some participants commented that they saw the risk of elevated inflation as remaining more prominent, or as having diminished by less, than risks to employment
current monetary policy moderately or modestly restrictive
dovish_phrases: Most participants assessed that some reduction in the target range for the federal funds rate this year would likely be appropriate

A couple of participants noted that, if the data evolve in line with their expectations, they would be open to considering a reduction in the target range for the policy rate as soon as at the next meeting
uncertainty about the economic outlook had diminished
inflation had eased significantly since its peak in 2022
A few participants saw risks to the labor market as having become predominant
summary: The FOMC unanimously held rates at 4.25-4.5% with most participants seeing some rate cuts likely appropriate this year, though uncertainty remains elevated and inflation is still somewhat elevated, creating a mildly hawkish hold with mixed forward guidance.
